response.justification: A strong, versatile engineer with proven experience on complex, large-scale systems (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/sec) and genuine, directly relevant exposure to IDE internals and language tooling (C/C++ work on NI LabWindows/CVI, plus authoring test runners and assertion libraries). However, the core technical requirements are a poor match: the candidate's depth is in TypeScript/Node and DLang, with Java listed only as a peripheral skill, no demonstrated Kotlin, and no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate ecosystem experience. The JVM-specific bar is not met, so this is a borderline candidate worth a screening conversation only if JVM depth can be validated.
strengths: Real IDE-internals and language-tooling experience (C IDE work, test runners, libraries)
Proven on complex, high-performance, large-scale codebases
Strong software-craft practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin and only shallow, peripheral Java experience
Core depth is in TypeScript/Node and DLang, not the JVM
